req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 4
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path bulk import where all rows pass validation and are committed, (2) partial import where some rows fail validation and an error report is returned, (3) rejection of non-CSV MIME types (415), (4) rejection of CSVs missing required headers (422), and (5) rate-limiting per user. Tests are derived using EP for normal partitions, NEG for error pathways, BVA for the boundary between zero-error and one-error rows, DT for the combined header/MIME decision space, and STC for the upload → validate → commit state flow. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that authenticate as hr-admin, upload a file, and tear down imported records.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed
preconditions: CSV contains 3 rows all with valid data and required headers
Employees in the CSV do not already exist in the system
actions: Construct a valid CSV with headers: id,first_name,last_name,email,department
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 status code
Response body reports 3 rows imported, 0 errors
Each employee record is retrievable via GET /employees/{id}
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_all_valid_rows_committed(hr_admin_client, cleanup_employees):
    csv_content = (
        "id,first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        "E001,Alice,Smith,alice@example.com,Engineering\n"
        "E002,Bob,Jones,bob@example.com,HR\n"
        "E003,Carol,White,carol@example.com,Finance\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported"] == 3
    assert body["errors"] == []

    cleanup_employees(["E001", "E002", "E003"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_partial_commit_with_error_report
preconditions: CSV contains 2 valid rows and 1 invalid row (missing email)
Valid employees do not already exist in the system
actions: Construct a CSV with 2 valid rows and 1 row missing the email field
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 207 or 200 status code indicating partial success
Response body reports 2 rows imported
Error report contains exactly 1 entry referencing the invalid row number
Only the 2 valid employees are retrievable via GET /employees
trace: R-WEB-100 — commit only rows that pass validation
R-WEB-100 — error report generated per row
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_partial_commit_with_error_report(hr_admin_client, cleanup_employees):
    csv_content = (
        "id,first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        "E010,Dan,Brown,dan@example.com,Sales\n"
        "E011,Eve,Black,,Marketing\n"
        "E012,Frank,Green,frank@example.com,IT\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported"] == 2
    assert len(body["errors"]) == 1
    assert body["errors"][0]["row"] == 3

    cleanup_employees(["E010", "E012"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_rejects_non_csv_file_with_415
preconditions: File to upload is a JSON file, not a CSV
actions: Construct a JSON payload that resembles employee data
POST the JSON file to /employees/import with Content-Type: application/json
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type status code
Response body contains an error message indicating only CSV is accepted
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_rejects_non_csv_file_with_415(hr_admin_client):
    json_content = '[{"id":"E099","first_name":"Ghost"}]'
    files = {"file": ("employees.json", json_content, "application/json")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.json()
    assert "csv" in body.get("detail", "").lower() or "unsupported" in body.get("detail", "").lower()

tests.name: test_bulk_import_rejects_csv_missing_required_header_with_422
preconditions: CSV is well-formed but is missing the required 'email' column header
actions: Construct a CSV that omits the 'email' header column
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity status code
Response body identifies 'email' as a missing required column
trace: R-WEB-100 — 422 when header missing required columns
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_rejects_csv_missing_required_header_with_422(hr_admin_client):
    csv_content = (
        "id,first_name,last_name,department\n"
        "E020,Hank,Pym,Science\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    detail = str(body.get("detail", "")).lower()
    assert "email" in detail

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_row_boundary_commit
preconditions: CSV contains exactly 1 valid data row (minimum non-empty import)
actions: Construct a CSV with the required headers and exactly 1 valid data row
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 200 or 201 status code
Response body reports exactly 1 row imported and 0 errors
The employee is retrievable via GET /employees/{id}
trace: BVA lower boundary: single data row is the minimum meaningful import
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_single_row_boundary_commit(hr_admin_client, cleanup_employees):
    csv_content = (
        "id,first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        "E030,Ivy,Reed,ivy@example.com,Legal\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["imported"] == 1
    assert body["errors"] == []

    cleanup_employees(["E030"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_rate_limit_enforced_after_threshold
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold for /employees/import is known (e.g. N requests per window)
User has not yet consumed any rate-limit quota in the current window
actions: Send N valid CSV import requests in rapid succession
Send one additional (N+1) import request
Capture the HTTP response for the N+1 request
expected: First N requests return HTTP 200 or 201
The N+1 request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response contains Retry-After header or equivalent rate-limit metadata
trace: R-WEB-100 — operation must be rate-limited per user
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_rate_limit_threshold

def test_bulk_import_rate_limit_enforced_after_threshold(hr_admin_client, reset_rate_limit):
    threshold = get_rate_limit_threshold("/employees/import")
    csv_content = (
        "id,first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
        "E040,Jack,Fox,jack@example.com,Ops\n"
    )
    files = lambda: {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}

    for i in range(threshold):
        r = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files())
        assert r.status_code in (200, 201, 207)

    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files())
    assert resp.status_code == 429
    assert "retry-after" in resp.headers or "x-ratelimit-reset" in resp.headers

tests.name: test_bulk_import_empty_csv_body_returns_error
preconditions: CSV file contains only the header row with no data rows
actions: Construct a CSV with valid headers but zero data rows
POST the CSV file to /employees/import with Content-Type: text/csv
Capture the HTTP response
expected: HTTP 200 or 422 status code
Response body reports 0 rows imported
No records are created in the system
trace: R-WEB-100 — valid rows imported (zero is the lower boundary)
BVA: header-only CSV is the zero-data boundary case
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_bulk_import_empty_csv_body_returns_error(hr_admin_client):
    csv_content = "id,first_name,last_name,email,department\n"
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", csv_content, "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_client.post("/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("imported", 0) == 0
